rxn_class_summary: Electroreductive Ni-catalyzed cyclopropanation of alkenes using dichloromethane as a carbene precursor (with a related β-methylation side pathway)
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of Ni(II)/iPr-PyBOX to Ni(0), which mediates the reduction/activation of dichloromethane to generate a Ni-carbene species; olefin coordination further facilitates DCM reduction

key_intermediates.name: Cyclopropane product (e.g., 29b) – not on-cycle intermediate for methylation

electron_flow: Cathode reduces Ni(II)(iPr-PyBOX) to Ni(0); the Ni(0) complex reduces/activates CH2Cl2 to form a Ni-carbene, whose addition across the alkene delivers the cyclopropane; binding of the olefin lowers the DCM reduction onset, enabling catalyst turnover.
rate_determining_step: Ni-mediated reduction/activation of dichloromethane (onset shifted from -2.48 V to -1.91 V vs Fc/Fc+ in presence of Ni and olefin)

probe_reagents.name: CD2Cl2 (d2-dichloromethane)
probe_reagents.observation: β-methylation of 25a gives CD2H-incorporated product (48% qNMR), confirming the methylene of DCM is transferred into the methyl product

probe_reagents.name: Cyclopropane substrate 29b (stability test)
probe_reagents.observation: 29b recovered in 61% with 0% methylation product 1c, showing cyclopropane is not an intermediate to the methylated product (off-cycle pathway)

probe_reagents.name: On/off current experiment (substrate 8b)
probe_reagents.observation: Yield increases only while current is on and plateaus when off across 3 cycles, indicating continuous electrolysis is required (no radical chain)

probe_reagents.name: Dichlorodiphenylmethane (CV probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Irreversible cathodic peak at -2.34 V vs Fc/Fc+; direct reduction to tetraphenylethylene unlikely, supporting Ni-carbene homocoupling pathway

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of Ni/iPr-PyBOX ± DCM ± olefin 6a
probe_reagents.observation: DCM reduction onset shifts from -2.48 V (no Ni) to -2.00 V (with Ni) to -1.91 V (with Ni and 6a), supporting Ni-mediated reduction of DCM enhanced by olefin binding

probe_reagents.name: (S)-iPr-PyBOX chiral ligand
probe_reagents.observation: 44% ee in product 1b, indicating a chiral Ni complex is involved in the stereodetermining C-C bond formation

notes: Two product manifolds are observed: on-cycle cyclopropanation via a Ni-carbene from CH2Cl2 + alkene, and an off-cycle β-methylation pathway that does NOT proceed through the cyclopropane intermediate (shown by stability of 29b under reaction conditions). CD2Cl2 labeling confirms DCM-derived CH2 is incorporated into both the cyclopropane and methyl products. CV data support that the olefin assists regeneration/turnover of the active Ni species for DCM activation. Alternative direct cathodic reduction of a dichloro intermediate to a free carbenoid is disfavored by CV of dichlorodiphenylmethane.
